name: pyln-client

metadata.name: pyln-client
metadata.summary: Client library and plugin library for Core Lightning

documentation:
# pyln-client: A python client library for lightningd

This package implements the Unix socket based JSON-RPC protocol that
`lightningd` exposes to the rest of the world. It can be used to call
arbitrary functions on the RPC interface, and serves as a basis for plugins
written in python.


## Installation

`pyln-client` is available on `pip`:

```
pip install pyln-client
```

Alternatively you can also install the development version to get access to
currently unreleased features by checking out the Core Lightning source code and
installing into your python3 environment:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ElementsProject/lightning.git
cd lightning/contrib/pyln-client
poetry install
```

This will add links to the library into your environment so changing the
checked out source code will also result in the environment picking up these
changes. Notice however that unreleased versions may change API without
warning, so test thoroughly with the released version.

## Examples


### Using the JSON-RPC client
```py
"""
Generate invoice on one daemon and pay it on the other
"""
from pyln.client import LightningRpc
import random

# Create two instances of the LightningRpc object using two different Core Lightning daemons on your computer
l1 = LightningRpc("/tmp/lightning1/lightning-rpc")
l5 = LightningRpc("/tmp/lightning5/lightning-rpc")

info5 = l5.getinfo()
print(info5)

# Create invoice for test payment
invoice = l5.invoice(100, "lbl{}".format(random.random()), "testpayment")
print(invoice)

# Get route to l1
route = l1.getroute(info5['id'], 100, 1)
print(route)

# Pay invoice
print(l1.sendpay(route['route'], invoice['payment_hash']))
```

### Writing a plugin

Plugins are programs that `lightningd` can be configured to execute alongside
the main daemon. They allow advanced interactions with and customizations to
the daemon.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
from pyln.client import Plugin

plugin = Plugin()

@plugin.method("hello")
def hello(plugin, name="world"):
    """This is the documentation string for the hello-function.

    It gets reported as the description when registering the function
    as a method with `lightningd`.

    If this returns (a dict), that's the JSON "result" returned.  If
    it raises an exception, that causes a JSON "error" return (raising
    pyln.client.RpcException allows finer control over the return).
    """
    greeting = plugin.get_option('greeting')
    s = '{} {}'.format(greeting, name)
    plugin.log(s)
    return s


@plugin.init()
def init(options, configuration, plugin):
    plugin.log("Plugin helloworld.py initialized")
    # This can also return {'disabled': <reason>} to self-disable,
	# but normally it returns None.


@plugin.subscribe("connect")
def on_connect(plugin, connect, **kwargs):
    plugin.log("Received connect event for peer {}".format(connect))


plugin.add_option('greeting', 'Hello', 'The greeting I should use.')
plugin.run()

```

class_variables.name: pyln.client.gossmap.LnFeatureBits.OPTION_ZEROCONF
documentation: IN

feature flags taken from bolts.git/09-features.md

Flags are numbered from the least-significant bit, at bit 0 (i.e. 0x1,
an _even_ bit). They are generally assigned in pairs so that features
can be introduced as optional (_odd_ bits) and later upgraded to be compulsory
(_even_ bits), which will be refused by outdated nodes:

CONTEXT:
* `I`: presented in the `init` message.
* `N`: presented in the `node_announcement` messages
* `C`: presented in the `channel_announcement` message.
* `C-`: presented in the `channel_announcement` message, but always odd (optional).
* `C+`: presented in the `channel_announcement` message, but always even (required).
* `9`: presented in [BOLT 11](11-payment-encoding.md) invoices.

   FEATURE_NAME                                    # CONTEXT   # PRs
   -----------------------------------------------------------------

Note: only one of `source` or `destination` can be given.

functions.name: pyln.client.clnutils.cln_parse_rpcversion
documentation:
Parse cln version string to determine RPC version.

cln switched from 'semver' alike    `major.minor.sub[rcX][-mod]`
to ubuntu style with version 22.11  `yy.mm[.patch][-mod]`
make sure we can read all of them for (the next 80 years).

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.Millisatoshi.to_satoshi_str

documentation: Return a string of form 12.34567890btc or 12.34567890123btc.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.Millisatoshi.to_approx_str
documentation:
Returns the shortmost string using common units representation.

Rounds to significant `digits`. Default: 3

A subtype to represent thousandths of a satoshi.

If you put this in an object, converting to JSON automatically makes it an "...msat" string, so you can safely hand it even to our APIs which treat raw numbers as satoshis.  Converts to and from int.

classes.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixSocket
documentation:
A wrapper for socket.socket that is specialized to unix sockets.

Some OS implementations impose restrictions on the Unix sockets.

 - On linux OSs the socket path must be shorter than the in-kernel buffer
   size (somewhere around 100 bytes), thus long paths may end up failing
   the `socket.connect` call.

This is a small wrapper that tries to work around these limitations.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc._readobj
documentation: Read a JSON object, starting with buff; returns object and any buffer left over.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc.__getattr__
documentation:
Intercept any call that is not explicitly defined and call @call.

We might still want to define the actual methods in the subclasses for
documentation purposes.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc.get_json_id
documentation: Get a nicely formatted, CLN-compliant JSON ID

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc.call
documentation:
Generic call API: you can set cmdprefix here, or set self.cmdprefix
before the call is made.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc.notify
documentation:
Register a notification callback to use for a set of RPC calls.

This is a context manager and should be used like this:

```python
def fn(message, progress, request, **kwargs):
    print(message)

with rpc.notify(fn):
    rpc.somemethod()
```

The `fn` function will be called once for each notification
the is sent by `somemethod`. This is a context manager,
meaning that multiple commands can share the same context, and
the same notification function.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.UnixDomainSocketRpc.reply_filter
documentation:
Filter the fields returned from am RPC call (or more than one)..

This is a context manager and should be used like this:

```python
with rpc.reply_filter({"transactions": [{"outputs": [{"amount_msat": true, "type": true}]}]}):
    rpc.listtransactions()
```

classes.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.addgossip
documentation: Inject this (hex-encoded) gossip message.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.autoclean_status

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.close
documentation:
Close the channel with peer {id}, forcing a unilateral
close after {unilateraltimeout} seconds if non-zero, and
the to-local output will be sent to {destination}.

If channel funds have been leased to the peer and the
lease has not yet expired, you can force a close with
{force_lease_closed}. Note that your funds will still be
locked until the lease expires.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.connect
documentation: Connect to {peer_id} at {host} and {port}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.datastore
documentation:
Add/replace an entry in the datastore; either string or hex.
{key} can be a single string, or a sequence of strings.
{mode} defaults to 'must-create', but other options are possible:
  - 'must-replace': fail it it doesn't already exist.
  - 'create-or-replace': don't fail.
  - 'must-append': must exist, and append to existing.
  - 'create-or-append': set, or append to existing.
{generation} only succeeds if the current entry has this generation count (mode must be 'must-replace' or 'must-append').

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.datastoreusage
documentation:
Returns the total bytes that are stored for under the given key or the
root of the datastore. All descendants of the given key (or root) are
taken into account.
{key} can be a single string or a sequence of strings.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.decodepay
documentation: Decode {bolt11}, using {description} if necessary.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.deldatastore
documentation:
Remove an existing entry from the datastore.
{key} can be a single string, or a sequence of strings.
{generation} means delete only succeeds if the current entry has this generation count.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.disconnect

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.feerates
documentation: Supply feerate estimates manually.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.fundchannel
documentation:
Fund channel with {id} using {amount} satoshis with feerate
of {feerate} (uses default feerate if unset).
If {announce} is False, don't send channel announcements.
Only select outputs with {minconf} confirmations.
If {utxos} is specified (as a list of 'txid:vout' strings),
fund a channel from these specifics utxos.
{close_to} is a valid Bitcoin address.

{request_amt} is the lease amount to request from the peer. Only
valid if peer is advertising a liquidity ad + supports v2 channel opens
(dual-funding)

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.fundchannel_start
documentation:
Start channel funding with {id} for {amount} satoshis
with feerate of {feerate} (uses default feerate if unset).
If {announce} is False, don't send channel announcements.
Returns a Bech32 {funding_address} for an external wallet
to create a funding transaction for. Requires a call to
'fundchannel_complete' to complete channel establishment
with peer.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.fundchannel_cancel
documentation: Cancel a 'started' fundchannel with node {id}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.fundchannel_complete
documentation: Complete channel establishment with {id}, using {psbt}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.getinfo
documentation: Show information about this node.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.getlog
documentation: Show logs, with optional log {level} (info|unusual|debug|io).

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.getpeer

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.getroute
documentation:
Show route to {id} for {amount_msat}, using {riskfactor} and optional
{cltv} (default 9). If specified search from {fromid} otherwise use
this node as source. Randomize the route with up to {fuzzpercent}
(0.0 -> 100.0, default 5.0). {exclude} is an optional array of
scid/direction or node-id to exclude. Limit the number of hops in the
route to {maxhops}.

Show outgoing payments, regarding {bolt11} or {payment_hash} if set
Can only specify one of {bolt11} or {payment_hash}. It is possible

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.listpeers
documentation: Show current peers, if {level} is set, include {log}s".

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.listpeerchannels

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.multifundchannel
documentation:
Fund channels to an array of {destinations},
each entry of which is a dict of node {id}
and {amount} to fund, and optionally whether
to {announce} and how much {push_msat} to
give outright to the node.
You may optionally specify {feerate},
{minconf} depth, and the {utxos} set to use
for the single transaction that funds all
the channels.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.plugin_start
documentation: Adds a plugin to lightningd.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.plugin_startdir
documentation: Adds all plugins from a directory to lightningd.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.plugin_stop
documentation: Stops a lightningd plugin, will fail if plugin is not dynamic.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.plugin_list
documentation: Lists all plugins lightningd knows about.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.sendcustommsg
documentation: Sending custom message {message} to {peer_id}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.sendpay
documentation: Send along {route} in return for preimage of {payment_hash}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.sendonion
documentation:
Send an outgoing payment using the specified onion.

This method allows sending a payment using an externally
generated routing onion, with optional metadata to facilitate
internal handling, but not required.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.setchannel
documentation:
Set configuration a channel/peer {id} (or 'all').

{feebase} is a value in millisatoshi that is added as base fee
to any routed payment.

{feeppm} is a value added proportionally per-millionths to any
routed payment volume in satoshi.

{htlcmin} is the minimum (outgoing) htlc amount to allow and
advertize.

{htlcmax} is the maximum (outgoing) htlc amount to allow and
advertize.

{enforcedelay} is the number of seconds before enforcing this
change.

{ignorefeelimits} is a flag to indicate peer can set any feerate (dangerous!)

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.stop
documentation: Shut down the lightningd process.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.waitanyinvoice
documentation:
Wait for the next invoice to be paid, after {lastpay_index}
(if supplied).
Fail after {timeout} seconds has passed without an invoice
being paid.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.waitblockheight
documentation: Wait for the blockchain to reach the specified block height.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.waitinvoice
documentation: Wait for an incoming payment matching the invoice with {label}.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.waitsendpay
documentation: Wait for payment for preimage of {payment_hash} to complete.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.withdraw
documentation:
Send to {destination} address {satoshi} (or "all")
amount via Bitcoin transaction. Only select outputs
with {minconf} confirmations.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.txprepare
documentation:
Prepare a Bitcoin transaction which sends to [outputs].
The format of output is like [{address1: amount1},
{address2: amount2}], or [{address: "all"}]).
Only select outputs with {minconf} confirmations.

Outputs will be reserved until you call txdiscard or txsend, or
lightningd restarts.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.txdiscard
documentation:
Cancel a Bitcoin transaction returned from txprepare. The outputs
it was spending are released for other use.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.txsend
documentation: Sign and broadcast a Bitcoin transaction returned from txprepare.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.reserveinputs
documentation: Reserve any inputs in this psbt.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.unreserveinputs
documentation: Unreserve (or reduce reservation) on any UTXOs in this psbt were previously reserved.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.fundpsbt
documentation: Create a PSBT with inputs sufficient to give an output of satoshi.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.addpsbtoutput
documentation: Create a PSBT with an output of amount satoshi leading to the on-chain wallet

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.utxopsbt
documentation: Create a PSBT with given inputs, to give an output of satoshi.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.signpsbt
documentation: Add internal wallet's signatures to PSBT

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.sendpsbt
documentation: Finalize extract and broadcast a PSBT

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.signmessage
documentation: Sign a message with this node's secret key.

methods.name: pyln.client.lightning.LightningRpc.checkmessage
documentation:
Check if a message was signed (with a specific key).
Use returned field ['verified'] to get result.

RPC client for the `lightningd` daemon.

This RPC client connects to the `lightningd` daemon through a unix
domain socket and passes calls through. Since some of the calls
are blocking, the corresponding python methods include an `async`
keyword argument. If `async` is set to true then the method
returns a future immediately, instead of blocking indefinitely.

This implementation is thread safe in that it locks the socket
between calls, but it does not (yet) support concurrent calls.

functions.name: pyln.client.plugin.monkey_patch
documentation:
Monkey patch stderr and stdout so we use notifications instead.

A plugin commonly communicates with lightningd over its stdout and
stdin filedescriptor, so if we use them in some other way
(printing, logging, ...) we're breaking our communication
channel. This function monkey patches these streams in the `sys`
module to be redirected to a `PluginStream` which wraps anything
that would get written to these streams into valid log
notifications that can be interpreted and printed by `lightningd`.

classes.name: pyln.client.plugin.Method
documentation:
Description of methods that are registered with the plugin.

These can be one of the following:

 - RPC exposed by RPC passthrough
 - HOOK registered to be called synchronously by lightningd

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.RpcException.__init__
documentation: -32600 == "Invalid Request"

classes.name: pyln.client.plugin.Request

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Request._notify
documentation:
Send a notification to the caller.

Can contain a variety of things, but is usually used to report
progress or command status.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Request.notify
documentation: Send a message notification to the caller.
A request object that wraps params and allows async return

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Option.__getitem__
documentation: Backwards compatibility for callers who directly asked for ['value']

classes.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_method
documentation:
Add a plugin method to the dispatch table.

The function will be expected at call time (see `_dispatch`)
and the parameters from the JSON-RPC call will be mapped to
the function arguments. In addition to the parameters passed
from the JSON-RPC call we add a few that may be useful:

 - `plugin`: gets a reference to this plugin.

 - `request`: gets a reference to the raw request as a
   dict. This corresponds to the JSON-RPC message that is
   being dispatched.

Notice that due to the python binding logic we may be mapping
the arguments wrongly if we inject the plugin and/or request
in combination with positional binding. To prevent issues the
plugin and request argument should always be the last two
arguments and have a default on None.

The `background` argument can be used to specify whether the method is
going to return a result that should be sent back to the lightning
daemon (`background=False`) or whether the method will return without
sending back a result. In the latter case the method MUST use
`request.set_result` or `result.set_exception` to return a result or
raise an exception for the call.

`deprecated` True means that it won't appear unless `allow-deprecated-apis`
is true (the default), or if list of version string (e.g. "v23.08"), then
start deprecation cycle at that version (and removal after second entry in list).

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_subscription
documentation:
Add a subscription to our list of subscriptions.

A subscription is an association between a topic and a handler
function. Adding a subscription means that we will
automatically subscribe to events from that topic with
`lightningd` and, upon receiving a matching notification, we
will call the associated handler. Notice that in order for the
automatic subscriptions to work, the handlers need to be
registered before we send our manifest, hence before
`Plugin.run` is called.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.subscribe
documentation: Function decorator to register a notification handler.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_option
documentation:
Add an option that we'd like to register with lightningd.

Needs to be called before `Plugin.run`, otherwise we might not
end up getting it set.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_flag_option
documentation:
Add a flag option that we'd like to register with lightningd.

Needs to be called before `Plugin.run`, otherwise we might not
end up getting it set.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_notification_topic
documentation: Announce that the plugin will emit notifications for the topic.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.async_method
documentation:
Decorator to add an async plugin method to the dispatch table.

Internally uses add_method.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.method
documentation:
Decorator to add a plugin method to the dispatch table.

Internally uses add_method.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.add_hook
documentation: Register a hook that is called synchronously by lightningd on events

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.hook

Decorator to add a plugin hook to the dispatch table.

Internally uses add_hook.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin.async_hook
documentation:
Decorator to add an async plugin hook to the dispatch table.

Internally uses add_hook.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin._multi_dispatch
documentation:
We received a couple of messages, now try to dispatch them all.

Returns the last partial message that was not complete yet.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.Plugin._set_config
documentation: Called when the value of a dynamic option is changed

Controls interactions with lightningd, and bundles functionality.

The Plugin class serves two purposes: it collects RPC methods and
options, and offers a control loop that dispatches incoming RPC
calls and hooks.

classes.name: pyln.client.plugin.PluginStream
documentation: Sink that turns everything that is written to it into a notification.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.PluginLogHandler.emit
documentation:
Emit a record.

If a formatter is specified, it is used to format the record. Numeric
levels are translated into strings that lightningd understands. If
exception information is present, it is formatted using
traceback.print_exception and appended to the stream.

methods.name: pyln.client.plugin.PluginLogHandler.flush
documentation: Flushing is a no-op since each message is written as it comes in.
